_comment: Skill Graph rebuild Phase 3 (2026-07-23) — canonical B1 curriculum. Sourced from the standard CEFR progression, sequenced the way mainstream coursebooks order B1 topics — informed by their structure, not copied text. Cross-links back to real a1.json/a2.json keys where B1 genuinely builds on an earlier foundation. Consumed by POST /api/admin/skill-graph/nodes/import.

nodes.title: Past perfect simple
nodes.description: Clarifying which of two past events happened first.
nodes.descriptionForAi: Cross-level: builds on A2's past simple/continuous.

nodes.title: Combining narrative tenses to tell a story
nodes.description: Weaving past simple, past continuous, and past perfect together fluently.

nodes.title: Second conditional (hypothetical present/future)
nodes.description: If + past simple, would + base verb for unreal or unlikely situations.

nodes.title: Present perfect continuous
nodes.description: Emphasizing the duration of an activity that started in the past and continues.

nodes.title: Present perfect vs. past simple
nodes.description: Choosing correctly between a finished-time past and an unfinished-time perfect.

nodes.title: Passive voice: present simple
nodes.description: Shifting focus from the agent to the action/object in the present.

nodes.title: Passive voice: past simple
nodes.description: Using the passive to describe a completed past action without naming who did it.

nodes.title: Reported speech: statements
nodes.description: Backshifting tense to report what someone said.

nodes.title: Reported speech: questions
nodes.description: Reporting a question someone asked, with correct word order.

nodes.title: Defining relative clauses
nodes.description: Using who/which/that to add essential information to a noun.

nodes.title: Modals of deduction: present (must/might/can't be)
nodes.description: Speculating about the present with confidence levels.

nodes.title: Modals of deduction: past (must/might/can't have)
nodes.description: Speculating about the past with confidence levels.

nodes.title: Used to for past habits and states
nodes.description: Contrasting a repeated past habit with the present.

nodes.title: Gerunds vs. infinitives after verbs
nodes.description: Knowing which verbs take -ing and which take to + verb.

nodes.title: Indirect questions for politeness
nodes.description: Softening a direct question (Could you tell me where...?).

nodes.title: As...as comparative structures
nodes.description: Comparing equality/inequality (as tall as, not as expensive as).

nodes.title: Few/a few, little/a little
nodes.description: Distinguishing negative and positive quantity nuances.

nodes.title: Linking words of contrast (although, however, despite)
nodes.description: Joining contrasting ideas formally in speech and writing.

nodes.title: Separable and inseparable phrasal verbs
nodes.description: Knowing when an object can split a phrasal verb and when it can't.

nodes.title: Future continuous
nodes.description: Describing an action that will be in progress at a specific future time.

nodes.title: Introduction to third conditional
nodes.description: Expressing past regrets and alternate histories (if I had known...).

nodes.title: Causative have/get something done
nodes.description: Describing arranging for someone else to do something for you.

nodes.title: Environment and climate
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing environmental issues and climate change.

nodes.title: Relationships and dating
nodes.description: Vocabulary for talking about friendships, dating, and relationships.

nodes.title: Crime and law
nodes.description: Basic crime, police, and legal-system vocabulary.

nodes.title: Advertising and consumerism
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing ads, brands, and consumer habits.

nodes.title: Global issues
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing world problems (poverty, inequality, health).

nodes.title: Describing trends and graphs
nodes.description: Vocabulary for describing rises, falls, and trends in data.

nodes.title: Job interviews and applications
nodes.description: Vocabulary for CVs, cover letters, and interview questions.

nodes.title: Housing and renting
nodes.description: Vocabulary for renting, leases, and describing accommodation.

nodes.title: Social media and communication
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing social media habits and online communication.

nodes.title: Common everyday idioms
nodes.description: Frequent idiomatic expressions used in daily conversation.

nodes.title: Collocations with make and do
nodes.description: Common fixed pairings (make a decision, do homework).

nodes.title: Describing processes and sequences
nodes.description: Vocabulary for describing how something is made or done step by step.

nodes.title: Travel problems and complaints
nodes.description: Vocabulary for describing travel problems and making complaints.

nodes.title: Cultural differences
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing customs, traditions, and cultural comparison.

nodes.title: Arts and literature
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing books, art, and creative works.

nodes.title: Science and innovation
nodes.description: Vocabulary for discussing scientific discoveries and new technology.

nodes.title: Personal finance (advanced)
nodes.description: Vocabulary for budgeting, saving, and financial decisions.

nodes.title: Workplace communication
nodes.description: Vocabulary for meetings, emails, and professional interactions.

nodes.title: Food and cooking (advanced)
nodes.description: More detailed cooking methods, textures, and flavor vocabulary.

nodes.title: Describing change over time
nodes.description: Vocabulary for describing how something has changed (increasingly, gradually).

nodes.title: Reading an intermediate newspaper article
nodes.description: Understanding a short news article on a familiar current topic.

nodes.title: Reading an opinion column
nodes.description: Understanding a writer's argument and supporting points.

nodes.title: Reading a formal letter of complaint
nodes.description: Understanding the structure and tone of a formal complaint letter.

nodes.title: Reading job advertisements and applications
nodes.description: Understanding a job ad and matching it against a CV.

nodes.title: Reading a short story with a flashback
nodes.description: Following a narrative that jumps between past perfect and simple past.

nodes.title: Identifying the writer's attitude
nodes.description: Recognizing whether a writer is critical, supportive, or neutral.

nodes.title: Understanding graphs and charts referenced in text
nodes.description: Connecting written description to the data shown in a simple chart.

nodes.title: Combining skimming and scanning strategies
nodes.description: Switching between fast gist-reading and detail-hunting in the same text.

nodes.title: Identifying cohesive devices
nodes.description: Recognizing linking words and reference words that hold a text together.

nodes.title: Reading for specific information (exam style)
nodes.description: Locating specific facts quickly in an exam-style reading task.

nodes.title: Understanding a debate or discussion
nodes.description: Following multiple speakers with different opinions.

nodes.title: Understanding spoken news reports
nodes.description: Following a short radio/TV-style news report.

nodes.title: Following a short lecture or talk
nodes.description: Understanding the main points of a short informational talk.

nodes.title: Understanding reported speech in conversation
nodes.description: Recognizing when a speaker is reporting someone else's words.

nodes.title: Identifying specific information in interviews
nodes.description: Picking out key facts from a longer, more natural-paced interview.

nodes.title: Understanding different English accents (basic)
nodes.description: Following clear speech in a few common non-standard accents.

nodes.title: Predicting content before listening
nodes.description: Using context clues to predict what a recording will be about.

nodes.title: Understanding a podcast extract
nodes.description: Following a short, informal spoken podcast segment.

nodes.title: Note-taking while listening
nodes.description: Writing down key points while a recording plays, without losing the thread.

nodes.title: Understanding speaker certainty and doubt
nodes.description: Recognizing hedging and certainty language in spoken English.

nodes.title: Writing a formal letter of complaint
nodes.description: Writing a structured formal complaint with a clear request for action.

nodes.title: Writing a for-and-against essay
nodes.description: Presenting both sides of an argument in a structured short essay.

nodes.title: Writing a job application letter
nodes.description: Writing a structured formal letter/email applying for a job.

nodes.title: Writing a story using narrative tenses
nodes.description: A short story combining past simple, continuous, and perfect naturally.

nodes.title: Summarizing a short text
nodes.description: Writing a concise summary that captures the main points only.

nodes.title: Writing a short report describing trends
nodes.description: Describing data trends shown in a graph or chart in formal writing.

nodes.title: Writing an online forum post
nodes.description: Writing an informal post asking for or giving advice online.

nodes.title: Expressing hypothetical situations in writing
nodes.description: Writing about imaginary present/future situations using second conditional.

nodes.title: Giving detailed opinions with justification
nodes.description: Writing an opinion paragraph supported by clear reasons and examples.

nodes.title: Paraphrasing simple sentences
nodes.description: Rewriting a sentence with the same meaning using different structures.

nodes.title: Discussing global issues
nodes.description: Talking about a world problem and possible solutions.

nodes.title: Speculating about past and present situations
nodes.description: Guessing what happened or what's true using modals of deduction.

nodes.title: Describing hypothetical situations aloud
nodes.description: Talking about imaginary present/future situations using second conditional.

nodes.title: Job interview roleplay
nodes.description: A mock job interview practicing common questions and polite forms.

nodes.title: Reporting what someone said aloud
nodes.description: Relaying a conversation to a third person using reported speech.

nodes.title: Discussing advantages and disadvantages
nodes.description: Weighing the pros and cons of a topic aloud.

nodes.title: Talking about life changes
nodes.description: Contrasting past habits with present life using used to and phrasal verbs.

nodes.title: Negotiating and compromising
nodes.description: Reaching an agreement through polite negotiation language.

nodes.title: Describing a process aloud
nodes.description: Explaining how something is made or done, step by step.

nodes.title: Complaining politely in person
nodes.description: A roleplay making a spoken complaint while staying polite.

nodes.title: Discussing cultural differences
nodes.description: Comparing customs and traditions across cultures in conversation.

nodes.title: Giving a short prepared talk
nodes.description: Delivering a short, structured spoken presentation on a familiar topic.

nodes.title: Stress in multi-syllable words
nodes.description: Extending word-stress rules to longer, more complex words.

nodes.title: Intonation for expressing certainty and doubt
nodes.description: Using intonation to signal how confident you are about a claim.

nodes.title: Weak and strong forms of function words
nodes.description: Distinguishing reduced vs. emphasized pronunciation of function words.

nodes.title: Stress for emphasis in sentences
nodes.description: Shifting sentence stress to emphasize a particular word/idea.

nodes.title: Connected speech: elision
nodes.description: Recognizing/producing dropped sounds in fast natural speech.

nodes.title: Recognizing regional accent features
nodes.description: Noticing systematic sound differences across common English accents.

nodes.title: Intonation in reported speech
nodes.description: Using flatter, more neutral intonation typical of reporting speech.

nodes.title: Speaking at length without notes
nodes.description: Sustaining a spoken turn of 1-2 minutes without written support.

nodes.title: Using discourse markers (well, actually, anyway)
nodes.description: Using natural spoken discourse markers to manage a conversation.

nodes.title: Paraphrasing when stuck for a word
nodes.description: Describing around a missing word instead of stopping.

nodes.title: Maintaining a longer discussion
nodes.description: Sustaining a multi-turn discussion on an abstract or opinion-based topic.

nodes.title: Reducing reliance on memorized phrases
nodes.description: Generating original language instead of only reusing fixed chunks.

nodes.title: Defending an opinion under challenge
nodes.description: Holding your position politely when someone disagrees firmly.

nodes.title: Speaking in front of a small group
nodes.description: Delivering a short spoken turn to more than one listener.

nodes.title: Tolerating ambiguity in unfamiliar topics
nodes.description: Staying in a conversation even when not every word is understood.

nodes.title: Self-assessing fluency vs. accuracy trade-offs
nodes.description: Reflecting on when to prioritize speaking smoothly vs. speaking correctly.

nodes.title: Handling unexpected questions
nodes.description: Responding calmly to a question you didn't prepare for.
